title: Sanji Shows the Baratie’s True Code
description: On pages 9–12, Sanji feeds Gin despite the danger, revealing that his compassion for the hungry matters more to him than restaurant rules or pirate labels.
scene: Gin arrives broken and starving, while the Baratie cooks judge him as an enemy; Sanji alone treats hunger as something that must be answered.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You're a pirate?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So why'd you try to fire a cannonball at this restaurant?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: oh that was just an accident. It was just a cannonball that went astray from my self-defence.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What's that supposed to mean?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: In any case, you'd better not try anything funny to this restaurant.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The owner of this place used to be the cook of a pretty infamous pirate crew.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Oh, that old man used to be a pirate?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This restaurant's like a treasure to that damn geezer.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: And the rest of the cooks who came to work here out of their admiration for that geezer are all hot-blooded and crude enough to be pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But it's quite appropriate considering all the pirates that frequent around these parts.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Not a quiet moment in this place, eh?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Yeah... but it's daily routine for us.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: There've even been some guests lately who come to see the pirates and the cooks Duke it out.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Shanks to that,

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: All the waiters got scared and ran away.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Ohhhh, so the reason he asked me to work here for a year was...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Eh whatever. Hey, join my crew.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'll have to refuse on that offer.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I've got my own reason to keep working here.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: No!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I refuse!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: WHA...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I refuse your refusal!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You're a good cook so we'll be pirates together!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey hey, at least hear me out...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Fine then, what's your reason?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: No need to tell you.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But you just told me to hear you out!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: All I meant was that you should hear other people's opinions when talking! Don't make me chop you up, you damn strawhat-wearing shithead!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What did you say!? Don't you dare insult my strawhat or I'll send you flying!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Sorry to cut in the conversation but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What is it!?

summary: This part reveals the core of Sanji’s morality: no matter who a person is, he cannot ignore someone starving in front of him.
